      "それはカウンターとして使用されるカウリ貝、種子、または小石で構成されています。",
      "それは2列と7列で構成されています。",
      "それは合計14個のカップと148個のカウンターで構成されています。",
      "それはカウンターとして種子、貝殻、小石が使われています。"

      "それは穴の配置にいくつかのバリエーションがあります。",
      "それは2列と7列の長方形のボードを持っています。",
      "それは合計14個のカップと148個のカウンターを持っています。",
      "それはプレイされているゲームのバリエーションによって異なるキャプチャールールを持っています。",
      "それは一人のプレイヤーがすべての貝殻をキャプチャしたときに勝者が宣言されます。",
      "それは各列の中央を除いて各カップに12個のカウンターが配置され、中央の列には2個のカウンターが配置されています。",
      "それはカウンターが残っていないときにラウンドが終了します。",
      "それはプレイヤーがすべての穴を埋めることができない場合にマークされるゴミ穴があります。",
      "それはプレイヤーがそれらを埋めるのに十分なカウンターを持っているときにゴミ穴のステータスが削除されます。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [

      "それは全部で14個の穴があることが特徴です。",
      "それは穴の配置にバリエーションがあることが特徴です。",

      "それは各プレイヤーが開始する貝殻の数によってバリエーションがあることが特徴です。",
      "それは2列と7列の長方形のボードであることが特徴です。",
      "それはカウンターとして種子、貝殻、小石が使用されることが特徴です。",
      "それはルールで許可されているようにプレイヤーが貝殻をキャプチャすることが特徴です。",
      "それは一人のプレイヤーがすべての貝殻をキャプチャしたときにゲームが終了することが特徴です。"
    ],
    "HeldIn": [
      "それはインドのカルナータカ州とアーンドラ・プラデーシュ州で開催されました。",
      "それはスリランカで開催されました。",
      "それはマレーシアで開催されました。",

      "それはフィリピンで開催されました。",
      "それはシンガポールで開催されました。",
      "それはトリニダードで開催されました。",
      "それはガイアナで開催されました。"

      "それはpathinālam kuḻiとも呼ばれます。",
      "それは2人によってプレイされます。",
      "それは高齢者の間で人気があります。",
      "それは子供たちが数えることを学ぶために奨励されています。",

About: It is about a traditional ancient mancala game.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with South India, especially Tamil Nadu and Kerala.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a wooden board that has fourteen pits in all.
It consists of cowry shells, seeds or small pebbles used as counters.
It consists of 2 rows and 7 columns.
It consists of a total of 14 cups and 148 counters.
It consists of seeds, shells, and small stones as counters.
CreatedFor: It was created for kids to learn to count.
It was created for kids to improve eye–hand coordination and concentration.
HasA: It has a wooden board that has fourteen pits in all.
It has several variations in the pit layout.
It has a rectangular board with 2 rows and 7 columns.
It has a total of 14 cups and 148 counters.
It has rules of capture that depend on the variant of the game played.
It has a winner declared when one player captures all the shells.
It has 12 counters placed in each cup except the middle of each row into which only 2 counters are placed.
It has a round that is over when no counters remain.
It has rubbish holes that are marked when a player is unable to fill all of his holes.
It has a status removed from rubbish holes when a player has enough counters to fill them.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being a traditional ancient mancala game.
It is characterized by being played by two players.
It is characterized by having fourteen pits in all.
It is characterized by having variations in the layout of the pits.
It is characterized by the use of cowry shells, seeds or small pebbles as counters.
It is characterized by variations depending on the number of shells each player starts with.
It is characterized by a rectangular board with 2 rows and 7 columns.
It is characterized by the use of seeds, shells, or small stones as counters.
It is characterized by players capturing shells as permitted by the rules.
It is characterized by the game ending when one player captures all the shells.
HeldIn: It was held in Karnataka and Andhra Pradesh in India.
It was held in Sri Lanka.
It was held in the Philippines.
IsA: It is a traditional ancient mancala game.
It is also called fourteen pits.
It is also called pathinālam kuḻi.
It is played by two players.
It is popular among the older generations of people.
It is encouraged for kids to learn to count.
It is encouraged for kids to improve eye–hand coordination and concentration.
It is played in Sri Lanka, Trinidad, Guyana and Singapore.
It is known as Congkak or Congklakin in Indonesia.
It is known as sungkâ in the Philippines.
KnownAs: It is known as fourteen pits.
It is known as pathinālam kuḻi.
It is known as Congkak or Congklakin in Indonesia.
It is known as sungkâ in the Philippines.
KnownFor: It is known for being a traditional ancient mancala game played in South India.
It is known for its variations in gameplay.
It is known for being popular among older generations and encouraged for children.
It is known for its introduction to Karnataka and Andhra Pradesh.
It is known for its introduction to Sri Lanka and Malaysia.
It is known for its introduction to Indonesia and the Philippines during Chola expeditions.
OccurredIn: It occurred in South India, especially Tamil Nadu and Kerala.
It occurred in Karnataka and Andhra Pradesh in India.
OriginatedIn: It originated in South India, especially Tamil Nadu and Kerala.
PerformedBy: It was performed by two players.
UsedAs: It is used as a pastime.
It is used as a way for older people to play with young family members.
UsedFor: It is used for capturing shells.
It is used for learning to count.
It is used for improving eye–hand coordination.
It is used for improving concentration.
It is used for filling holes.
It is used for marking rubbish holes.
It is used for filling rubbish holes back up.
UsedIn: It is used in South India, especially Tamil Nadu and Kerala.
It is used in Karnataka and Andhra Pradesh in India.
It is used in Sri Lanka.
It is used in the Philippines.
UsedTo: It is used to distribute counters.
It is used to capture counters.
It is used to fill holes.
It is used to mark rubbish holes.
It is used to remove the status of rubbish holes.

それはカウンターとして種子、貝殻、小石が使われています。

それは高齢者の間で人気があります。
それは子供たちが数えることを学ぶために奨励されています。

それはスリランカ、トリニダード、ガイアナ、シンガポールでプレイされています。
それはインドネシアではCongkakまたはCongklakinとして知られています。
それはフィリピンではsungkâとして知られています。
